title: Individuals, variables, and categorical & quantitative data
description: Practice identifying components of a data set: individuals, variables, categorical data, quantitative data.
topic_path: Individuals, variables, and categorical & quantitative data

assessment_items.question_content:
Millions of Americans rely on caffeine to get them up in the morning. Here's nutritional data on some popular drinks at Ben's Beans coffee shop:

Drink | Type | Calories | Sugars (g) | Caffeine (mg)
- | - | - | - | - |
**Brewed coffee** | Hot | $4$ | $0$ | $260$
**Caffè latte** | Hot | $100$ | $14$ | $75$
**Caffè mocha** | Hot | $170$ | $27$| $95$
**Cappuccino** | Hot | $60$ | $8$ | $75$
**Iced brewed coffee** | Cold | $60$ | $15$ | $120$
**Chai latte** | Hot | $120$ | $25$ | $60$ 

**The individuals in this data set are:**
[[☃ radio 3]]

**This data set contains:**
[[☃ radio 4]]
hints:
Individuals are the people or things described by a data set.

Variables are characteristics of the individuals that we measure or observe.

Categorical variables take on values that are labels or categories, and quantitative variables take on numerical values.

The individuals described by this data set are Ben's Beans drinks.

The characteristics listed—type, calories, sugars, caffeine—describe each of the drinks, not customers.

One of the variables—type—is categorical.

**The answers:**

**The individuals in this data set are Ben's Beans drinks.**

**This data set contains $4$ variables, $1$ of which is categorical.**

assessment_items.question_content:
Rachael gave a survey to her classmates to learn about their superstitions. Here's some of the data she collected.

Student name| Gender | Age | Birth month | Read horoscope?
- | - | - | - | - |
**Jacob** | Male | $17$ | April | yes
**Trenae** | Female | $17$ | January | no
**Maura** | Female | $16$ | February | no
... | ... | ... | ... | ...

**The individuals in this data set are:**
[[☃ radio 3]]

**This data set contains:**
[[☃ radio 4]]
hints:
Individuals are the people or things described by a data set.

Variables are characteristics of the individuals that we measure or observe.

Categorical variables take on values that are labels or categories, and quantitative variables take on numerical values.

The individuals described by this data set are Rachael's classmates.

The characteristics listed—gender, age, birth month, read horoscope—describe each of the students, not teachers.

One of the variables—age—is quantitative.

**The answers:**

**The individuals in this data set are Rachael's classmates.**

**This data set contains $4$ variables, $1$ of which is quantitative.**

assessment_items.question_content:
Mr. Gregory sold several cars this week. Here is some data about the cars he sold.

Car sold | Retail price | Miles per gallon | Seats | Navigation?
- | - | - | - | - | -
**Accord** |  $\$22,205$ |  $32$ | $5$ | no
**Altima** | $\$22,500$ | $32$ | $5$ | no
**Camry** |  $\$23,070$ | $30$ | $5$ | yes
... | ... | ... | ... | ...

**The individuals in this data set are:**
[[☃ radio 3]]

**This data set contains:**
[[☃ radio 4]]
hints:
Individuals are the people or things described by a data set.

Variables are characteristics of the individuals that we measure or observe.

Categorical variables take on values that are labels or categories, and quantitative variables take on numerical values.

The individuals described by this data set are cars Mr. Gregory sold.

The characteristics listed—retail price, miles per gallon, seats, and navigation—describe each of the cars, not customers.

Three of the variables—retail price, miles per gallon, and seats—are quantitative.

**The answers:**

**The individuals in this data set are cars.**

**This data set contains $4$ variables, $3$ of which are quantitative.**

assessment_items.question_content:
Fenyang is comparing rooms at a conference center. Here is some data he collected.

Room name | Seating capacity | Hire cost | Projector | Accessible
- | - | - | - | - |
**Boardroom** | $120$ | $\$400$ | yes | yes
**Bassford** |  $60$ | $\$300$ | yes | yes
**Fernery** | $200$ | $\$600$ | yes | yes
... | ... | ... | ... | ...

**The individuals in this data set are:**
[[☃ radio 3]]

**This data set contains:**
[[☃ radio 4]]
hints:
Individuals are the people or things described by a data set.

Variables are characteristics of the individuals that we measure or observe.

Categorical variables take on values that are labels or categories, and quantitative variables take on numerical values.

The individuals described by this data set are rooms at a conference center.

The characteristics listed—seating capacity, hire cost, projector, accessible—describe individual rooms, not entire conference centers.

Two of the variables—seating capacity and hire cost—are quantitative.

**The answers:**

**The individuals in this data set are rooms at a conference center.**

**This data set contains $4$ variables, $2$ of which are quantitative.**

assessment_items.question_content:
Camille is comparing different apartment complexes. Here is the data she collected.

Complex name | Bedrooms | Bathrooms | Rent | Rent includes heat | Amenities
- | - | - | - | - | - 
**Grand Vistas** | $2$ | $2$ | $990$ | no | fitness center, pool
**Maple Ridge** | $2$ | $1.5$ | $685$ | yes | fitness center, pool
**Old Mill** | $2$ | $1.5$ | $790$ | no | pool
**Willow Brook** | $2$ | $2$ | $885$ | no | jacuzzi

**The individuals in this data set are:**
[[☃ radio 3]]

**This data set contains:**
[[☃ radio 4]]
hints:
Individuals are the people or things described by a data set.

Variables are characteristics of the individuals that we measure or observe.

Categorical variables take on values that are labels or categories, and quantitative variables take on numerical values.

The individuals described by this data set are apartments.

The characteristics listed—bedrooms, bathrooms, rent, rent includes heat, amenities—describe each of the apartment complexes, not landlords.

Two of the variables—rent includes heat, and amenities—are categorical.

**The answers:**

**The individuals in this data set are apartment complexes.**

**This data set contains $5$ variables, $2$ of which are categorical.**

assessment_items.question_content:
Irene is gathering information about students at a local college. Here is some of the data she collected.

Student name | Class | Major | Dorm
- | - | - | - 
**Rosalyn** | Freshman | Psychology | Duncan Hall
**Marcelina** | Sophomore | Mathematics | Knott Hall
**Miles** | Freshman | Political Science | Lyons Hall
**Jacob** | Junior | Asian Literature | Dillon Hall
**Brayden** | Senior | Theater | Dillon Hall
**Samantha** | Sophomore | Electrical Engineering | Farley Hall
... | ... | ... | ...

**The individuals in this data set are:**
[[☃ radio 3]]

**This data set contains:**
[[☃ radio 4]]
hints:
Individuals are the people or things described by a data set.

Variables are characteristics of the individuals that we measure or observe.

Categorical variables take on values that are labels or categories, and quantitative variables take on numerical values.

The individuals described by this data set are college students.

The characteristics listed—class, major, dorm—describe each of the students, not the characteristics themselves.

All three of the variables—class, major, dorm—are categorical.

**The answers:**

**The individuals in this data set are college students.**

**This data set contains $3$ variables, $3$ of which are categorical.**

**Julia** | Ms. Andersen | 6 
... | ... | ...

**This data set contains:**
[[☃ radio 4]]
hints:
Individuals are the people or things described by a data set.

Variables are characteristics of the individuals that we measure or observe.

Categorical variables take on values that are labels or categories, and quantitative variables take on numerical values.

The individuals described by this data set are students.

The characteristics listed—homeroom teacher and absences—describe each of the students, not teachers or counselors.

assessment_items.question_content:
The girl's basketball team played a game this Wednesday. Here are some of the statistics for the players from the game.

Player name| Free throw percentage | Assists | Class
- | - | - | -
**Simone** | $100\%$ | $4$ | Junior
**Maricel** | $75\%$ | $3$ | Junior
**Derin** | $80\%$ | $7$ | Senior
**Alli** | $90\%$ | $2$ | Senior
**Asha** | $50\%$ | $0$ | Sophomore
... | ... | ... | ... 

**The individuals in this data set are:**
[[☃ radio 3]]

**This data set contains:**
[[☃ radio 4]]
hints:
Individuals are the people or things described by a data set.

Variables are characteristics of the individuals that we measure or observe.

Categorical variables take on values that are labels or categories, and quantitative variables take on numerical values.

The individuals described by this data set are basketball players.

The characteristics listed—free throw percentage, assists, and class—describe each of the players, not whole games.

Two of the variables—free throw percentage and assists—are quantitative.

**The answers:**

**The individuals in this data set are basketball players.**

**This data set contains $3$ variables, $2$ of which are quantitative.**

assessment_items.question_content:
Madison recorded weather conditions for some major cities on the same day. Here is some of the data she collected.

City name | High temp. (degrees Celsius) | Kind of precipitation | Average wind speed (kilometers per hour) | Severe weather alert?
-| - | - | - | -
**Detroit** | $26$ | rain | $15$ | no
**San Francisco** | $20$ | none | $17$ | no
**New York** | $20$ | rain | $6$ | yes
...| ... | ... | ... | ...

**The individuals in this data set are:**
[[☃ radio 3]]

**This data set contains:**
[[☃ radio 4]]
hints:
Individuals are the people or things described by a data set.

Variables are characteristics of the individuals that we measure or observe.

Categorical variables take on values that are labels or categories, and quantitative variables take on numerical values.

The individuals described by this data set are cities.

The characteristics listed—high temperature, kind of precipitation, average wind speed, severe weather alert—apply to each of the cities.

Two of the variables—kind of precipitation and severe weather alert—are categorical.

**The answers:**

**The individuals in this data set are cities.**

**This data set contains $4$ variables, $2$ of which are categorical.**

assessment_items.question_content:
Alek is taking an inventory of styles of compression bandages for work. Here is the data he has collected.

Style ID | Width (inches) | Total length (yards) | Color
- | - | - | - 
**001** | $1$ | $20$ |  tan
**002** | $1$ | $20$ | brown
**003** | $1$ | $10$ | red 
**004** | $1$ | $15$ | blue
**005** | $2$ | $35$ | tan
**006** | $2$ | $20$ | brown


**The individuals in this data set are:**
[[☃ radio 3]]

**This data set contains:**
[[☃ radio 4]]
hints:
Individuals are the people or things described by a data set.

Variables are characteristics of the individuals that we measure or observe.

Categorical variables take on values that are labels or categories, and quantitative variables take on numerical values.

The individuals described by this data set are styles of compression bandages.

The characteristics listed—width, total length, and color—describe each style of compression bandages, not nurses.

One of the variables—color—is categorical.

**The answers:**

**The individuals in this data set are compression bandage styles.**

**This data set contains $3$ variables, $1$ of which is categorical.**
